title: euphroniaceae
intro: The Euphorbiaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as spurge family, which is characterized by its diverse range of species, including trees, shrubs, and herbs. This family includes over 300 genera and 7,500 species worldwide, showcasing a wide variety of adaptations and growth habits. The Euphorbiaceae family is known for its unique characteristics, such as the presence of milky sap and often, compound leaves. Many species within this family have significant economic and ecological importance, serving as sources of food, medicine, and timber.
